nodes.label: API Pagination Envelope Convention
nodes.rationale: List responses use {items, total, page, page_size}. 'items' is the FastAPI community convention and matches DataTable expectations. 'total' (not 'count') is unambiguous on partial last pages. page is 1-indexed for UI display. page_size max 100.

nodes.label: Separate Manifest Endpoint Design
nodes.rationale: GET /trips/{id}/manifest is a separate endpoint from GET /trips/{id} because a manifest can contain 100+ Parcel rows. Including it in every trip detail response would bloat Active Trips list and driver home screen. Two frontend hooks (useTrip, useManifest) map to two endpoints.

nodes.label: Driver OTP Authentication
nodes.rationale: Drivers authenticate per-trip via two-step OTP (request OTP, then verify). They do not have persistent portal accounts. Guards and warehouse staff have no logins at all. This minimises onboarding friction at precincts where staff rotate frequently.

nodes.label: Seal Mismatch Detection (H4)
nodes.rationale: H4 compares incoming seal number against H2 committed seal number. Mismatch creates TripException(SEAL_MISMATCH, CRITICAL) and transitions Trip.status to exception_hold. Returns 200 not 4xx — the trip continues with a dispatcher alert. Seal intact is the highest individual fraud signal on arrival.

nodes.label: Three-Count Reconciliation (H5)
nodes.rationale: H5 unloading reconciles three independent counts: manifest origin count (from Parcel Perfect at H2), destination Parcel Perfect scan-in count, and driver visual count. Discrepancy raises an exception before trip closes. Arrival verification is primary evidence by design.

nodes.label: DEMO_MODE Auth Stub
nodes.rationale: When DEMO_MODE=True, get_current_dispatcher() returns a fixed stub UserRead without JWT validation. Real JWT auth replaces only the function body when the auth module lands — the endpoint import never changes. This decouples endpoint development from auth implementation.

nodes.label: Trip Creation (Handshake 0) Flow
nodes.rationale: POST /trips creates Trip row, TripTrailer rows, and H0 HandshakeEvent atomically. FK records validated before any DB writes. Journey lock hash computed after DB flush. Hedera anchor queued via Celery (not blocking). Pulsit device ID snapshot captured on TripTrailer to prevent retroactive device reassignment from altering the audit trail.

nodes.label: Connection-Level Transaction Rollback Test Pattern
nodes.rationale: Integration tests run inside a connection-level transaction rolled back after each test. Gives every test a clean DB state without truncating tables. Requires TEST_DATABASE_URL pointing at a throwaway PostgreSQL DB. Uses create_savepoint join_transaction_mode.

nodes.label: Driver PWA Static Export Constraint
nodes.rationale: output: 'export' in next.config.ts is required for Capacitor Android APK bundling. This makes all driver-pwa pages 'use client' — an explicit exception to the general rule of minimising client components. Server Components, server actions, and next/headers are incompatible with static export.

nodes.label: Shared Frontend Types Package
nodes.rationale: frontend/shared/ contains types, mocks, constants, tokens, z-index, and cn utility accessed by both dispatcher/ and driver-pwa/ via the @shared/* path alias. Prevents cross-surface import while enabling code sharing. This is the seam where mock state swaps for real-API state.

nodes.label: Fixture-to-API Swap Pattern
nodes.rationale: All frontend data flows through hooks (useTrip, useTrips, useManifest, etc.). Hooks consume mocks on v1. When backend ships, only the hook internals swap to lib/api/ modules — pages and components are untouched. Pages that import from lib/mocks/ directly are a code-review block.

nodes.label: Five Handshakes Trip Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: The five-handshake model (Trip Creation → Origin Gate-In → Loading → Origin Gate-Out → Destination Gate-In → Unloading/Closed) is the core domain abstraction. Each handshake produces a signed event; H0/H2/H5 are blockchain-anchored, H1/H3/H4 are feeders. Arrival verification is asymmetrically weighted over departure to maximise fraud detection signal.

nodes.label: Hedera HCS Blockchain Anchoring
nodes.rationale: Only SHA-256 hashes are anchored to Hedera HCS (never PII). H0 anchors journey lock hash; H2 anchors pickup receipt; H5 anchors delivery receipt. Checkpoints and exceptions are batched via Merkle trees to reduce Hedera transaction costs.

nodes.label: Trip Status State Machine
nodes.rationale: 10-state machine: created → origin_gate_in → loading → origin_gate_out → in_transit → dest_gate_in → unloading → closed, with cancelled and exception_hold reachable from any active state. The frontend spec originally defined only 6 states and must be corrected to match.

nodes.label: Merkle Batch Anchoring
nodes.rationale: Multiple checkpoints or exceptions are aggregated into a Merkle tree before a single Hedera anchor transaction. Reduces Hedera cost for high-frequency in-transit events.

nodes.label: UI-Driven Simulation Harness
nodes.rationale: Button-press handshake transitions for demo are sanctioned by Bruce (live geofence cannot be reproduced in classroom). Fault injection must go through real API endpoints — never DB edits at demo time (Ammar's requirement). A dev-only /dev/simulate/[tripId] page exposes both legitimate transitions and fault injectors.

nodes.label: TripStop Multi-Leg Data Model
nodes.rationale: Option C from kickoff: single client at Trip level but N pickup-stops and N delivery-stops via a new TripStop model. Promoted to Option B (full multi-leg/multi-client) in the iteration2_master_plan pending Bruce's BQ3 reply. Hub-and-spoke is common even on FTL.

nodes.label: SQLAlchemy 2.0 Mapped/mapped_column Models
nodes.contributor: All 19 tables defined using Mapped/mapped_column syntax; circular FKs broken with use_alter=True

nodes.label: Pydantic v2 Schemas with field_validator
nodes.contributor: DriverCreate id_number validator (13-digit SA ID), TripCreate arrival-after-departure, HandshakeEventCreate sequence 0-5, MerkleBatchLeafCreate source_type

nodes.label: Initial Alembic Migration (0001_initial_schema)
nodes.contributor: Single migration creates all 19 tables, indexes, PostgreSQL updated_at trigger

nodes.label: Merkle Batch — Checkpoint/Exception Aggregation
nodes.contributor: MerkleBatch aggregates checkpoints or exceptions before anchoring; MerkleBatchLeaf stores individual leaf hashes with source_type (checkpoint|exception|artifact)

nodes.label: use_alter=True — Circular FK Dependency Breaking
nodes.contributor: EvidenceArtifact.trip_id, BlockchainReceipt.trip_id, HandshakeEvent artifact FKs all use use_alter=True; evidence_artifacts created before trips in migration; FKs added via ALTER TABLE after all tables exist

nodes.label: frontend/shared/ — Cross-Surface Shared Code
nodes.contributor: Shared types, mocks, constants, utilities accessed via @shared/* path alias; dispatcher and driver-pwa share 11 type files and mock fixture data

nodes.label: Canonical Demo Trip TRP-2026-0041
nodes.contributor: JHB to DBN trip, driver S. Dlamini; must exist verbatim in mock data; used in all documentation as canonical demo trip

nodes.label: Design System v2 — Clean Modern Aesthetic
nodes.contributor: Replaces Industrial Brutalism; soft ambient shadows; rounded corners (8px cards, 12px pills); blue accent #0051d5 for verified/active/trusted states; glassmorphism on nav only; Inter font throughout; no hard offset shadows
nodes.norm_label: design system v2 — clean modern aesthetic

nodes.label: Glassmorphism Navigation (.glass-nav)
nodes.contributor: background: rgba(252,248,249,0.8) + backdrop-filter: blur(12px); applied only to sticky headers and bottom nav bars; never on cards or content areas

nodes.label: Dispatcher Portal Phase 1 — 13 Pages
nodes.contributor: DispatcherShell+Sidebar, Login, Active Trips (/), Trip Detail, Trip Creation, Fleet Vehicles, Fleet Drivers, Trip History, Exceptions list, Exception Detail, SLA Reports, Settings, 404/Error pages

nodes.label: resource_service.py — Read-only Query Service
nodes.contributor: list_drivers, list_vehicles, list_precincts, list_trips, get_trip_detail; batch queries to avoid N+1; layering: imports only db/, schemas/, core/exceptions

nodes.label: Frontend API Client (client.ts)
nodes.contributor: Native fetch wrapper; getToken() is no-op placeholder for auth teammate to replace; ApiError class; api.get/api.post typed helpers

nodes.label: DEMO_MODE — JWT Bypass for Local Dev
nodes.contributor: DEMO_MODE=True bypasses JWT verification entirely; Bearer demo token accepted; allows frontend integration testing without auth teammate's work
nodes.norm_label: demo_mode — jwt bypass for local dev

nodes.label: seed_demo.py — Dev Database Seed Script
nodes.contributor: Creates demo operator/client orgs, dispatcher user, 2 drivers, 2 vehicles, 2 precincts; idempotent (skips existing rows); run after alembic upgrade head with DEMO_MODE=true

nodes.label: DispatcherShell — Two-Layer Chrome
nodes.contributor: Dark bg-canvas (#0a0a0c) outer layer with 12px padding; bg-surf rounded-xl floating panel inside; sidebar 220px fixed; main content scrolls within panel

nodes.label: Dispatcher UI Primitives (TopBar, StatCard, SecHead)
nodes.contributor: TopBar: 60px page header strip replacing PageHeader; StatCard: metric tile with 28px value; SecHead: section header band with optional gradient action button

nodes.label: DriverCreateBody — Org-ID Injection Pattern
nodes.contributor: Separate DriverCreateBody for dispatcher-supplied fields; organization_id injected from JWT not accepted from client; mirrors DriverCreate validator

nodes.label: Journey Lock Hash
nodes.contributor: SHA-256 of canonical trip params at creation anchored to Hedera HCS; includes trip_id, order_number, driver_id, horse_id, trailer_ids, precincts, created_by_user_id, created_at; tamper detection: current hash != Hedera tx hash

nodes.label: POPIA Compliance — No PII on Blockchain
nodes.contributor: Personal data (names, ID numbers, phone, plain license number) never reaches Hedera; only SHA-256 hashes go on chain; license_number_sha256 used instead of plaintext
nodes.norm_label: popia compliance — no pii on blockchain

nodes.label: anchor_service.py — Hedera Anchor Orchestration
nodes.contributor: anchor_subject() canonicalizes payload, computes SHA-256, calls HederaService.submit_hash(), persists BlockchainReceipt; sync for demo, designed for async Celery later

nodes.label: verification_service.py — Tamper Detection
nodes.contributor: verify_subject() returns VerifyStatus: verified | db_mismatch | hedera_mismatch | no_receipt; reconstructs trip payload from DB, compares with stored hash, then queries Hedera mirror node

nodes.label: Vehicle/Driver Append-Only Event Log
nodes.contributor: VehicleEvent and DriverEvent tables record mutations as append-only diffs; event_type=created captures initial snapshot; critical-field changes anchored, cosmetic changes recorded but unanchored

nodes.label: Critical Fields — Blockchain Anchor Trigger
nodes.contributor: VEHICLE_CRITICAL_FIELDS: registration, licence_disc_expiry, vehicle_type, vin_number, pulsit_device_id, is_active; DRIVER_CRITICAL_FIELDS: license_number, license_expiry, is_active; diff_critical_fields returns changed fields dict

nodes.label: BlockchainBadge — Hedera Anchor UI Component
nodes.contributor: Passive badge showing anchor state (anchored|pending|failed|unanchored); anchored state links to Hashscan with topic_id and sequence_number; NEXT_PUBLIC_HEDERA_HASHSCAN_BASE env var

nodes.label: SubjectType — Polymorphic Blockchain Receipt
nodes.contributor: Extends BlockchainReceipt with subject_type/subject_id so trips, vehicles, drivers, and event rows share one table; trip_id becomes nullable; composite index on subject_type+subject_id

nodes.label: Trust Model — Evidence-of-Record vs Truth-of-Origin
nodes.contributor: FreightProof proves data not tampered after entry (oracle problem acknowledged); mitigations: five-handshake multi-party chain, IDVS identity verification, Pulsit GPS independent oracle; not built for demo

nodes.label: Client Portal — Read-Only Evidence Portal
nodes.contributor: Next.js 15 Server Components only; no write operations; one-time OTP auth for receivers; scaffolded in Sprint 3 after PDF export feature; read-only view of trip status and evidence

nodes.label: Capacitor 6 Android APK Build
nodes.contributor: next build produces out/; npx cap sync android copies into Android project; android/ directory committed to git; teammates open in Android Studio

nodes.label: Spikes — Throwaway Exploratory Code
